results.possibleIdentity: Jay Albrecht was a mechanical engineer and technical professional active in New York City during the 1960s and 1970s. He was employed by Columbia University, where he specialized in television communications. Albrecht was a volunteer for WBAI (a listener-supported Pacifica Radio station in New York), notably recording an informal gathering with then-Congressman John V. Lindsay at his home in 1962 to discuss political issues. He was also a participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. Within the E.A.T. technical services directory, he was indexed as a resource for artists requiring expertise in mechanical engineering and television communications.
